data.title: Legacy Cedar Fair GAAP EPS of -$0.91 misses by $0.62, revenue of $650M beats by $51.95M
data.description:
• Legacy Cedar Fair press release (FUN): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.91 misses by $0.62.
  • Revenue of $650M (-5.4% Y/Y) beats by $51.95M.
  • Attendance totaled 9.3 million guests, down 13% or approximately 1.4 million visitors compared with the fourth quarter of 2024 -- on a per operating day basis attendance was down 2% compared with the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Per capita spending(2) was $66.41, up 8% compared with the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA(1) totaled $165 million compared with Adjusted EBITDA of $209 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Operating days totaled 779, down 11%, compared with 878 days in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: Cenovus Energy reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Cenovus Energy press release (CVE): Q4 EPS of C$0.50
  • Revenue of C$12.93B (-14.7% Y/Y).

data.title: eBay stock soars on blowout results, dividend hike, and $1.2B Depop deal
data.description:
eBay (EBAY) on Wednesday after the closing bell reported a top-and-bottom-line beat for the fourth quarter, helped by its eBay Live holiday shopping events and AI initiatives, among other things, and the e-commerce giant provided a better-than-expected forecast for the current quarter and approved a $2B stock buyback.

Shares of the company are up more than 8% in early premarket trading on Thursday. 

In a separate press release, eBay announced that it will purchase the secondhand clothing marketplace Depop from Etsy (ETSY) for $1.2B in cash in a move to capture the Gen Z and Millennial customer base.

The company also hiked its cash dividend for the first quarter to $0.31 per share, from a previous payout of $0.29 per share. The dividend is payable on March 20 to stockholders of record as of March 6.

For Q1, eBay expects net revenue between $3B and $3.05B, ahead of the consensus estimate of $2.80B, and non-GAAP earnings per share of $1.53 to $1.59, ahead of the $1.48 per share estimate.

"We have built significant momentum across our strategic priorities, delivering meaningful growth and reinforcing our leadership in recommerce," CEO Jamie Iannone said. "As we continue to harness AI to elevate the customer experience worldwide, eBay is in the strongest position it has been in years."

In Q4, gross merchandise volume was $21.2B, up 10% on an as-reported basis and up 8% excluding forex impact.

Active buyers—buyers who paid for a transaction on the company's marketplace platforms in the past 12 months—were up 0.75% from last year at 135M.

Net income for the quarter was $525M, or $1.14 per share, compared to $680M, or $1.40 per share, for the same period last year, weighed down by higher operating expenses.

Excluding one-time items, earnings per share from continuing operations were $1.41, beating the average analyst expectation by 6 cents.

Net revenue was up 15% at $3B and topped estimates by at least $100M.

data.title: NiCE launches $600M share buyback program, shares rise
data.description:
• NiCE (NICE) on Thursday announced a new $600 million share repurchase program as it surpassed Street estimates for quarterly earnings, sending shares higher in premarket trading.
  • The execution of this program is subject to the issuance of the company’s audited annual financial report for the year 2025. This authorization reflects the company’s conviction in its long-term growth opportunity and durability of its cash flow generation, it said in a statement.
  • Following this authorization, NiCE currently has approximately $1 billion of total remaining share repurchase capacity (including previously authorized share repurchase programs that were not fully exhausted). The new share repurchase program has an indefinite term. 
  • NiCE (NICE) shares rose ~7% to $105.00 in early trading.

data.title: Broadstone Net Lease raises dividend by 1% to $0.2925
data.description:
• Broadstone Net Lease (BNL) declares $0.2925/share quarterly dividend, 0.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.2900.
  • Forward yield 6.0%
  • Payable April 15; for shareholders of record March 31; ex-div March 31.

data.title: Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals stock falls after pricing $55M public offering
data.description:
• Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals (EPRX) priced its previously announced public offering of 6.43 million common shares at $7.00 per share, along with 1.43 million pre-funded warrants, for gross proceeds of approximately $55 million before expenses.
  • Cantor and LifeSci Capital are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering. Bloom Burton and Craig-Hallum are also acting as co-managers for the offering.
  • Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals shares fell 9.3% to $7.40 in premarket trading.

data.title: Fortuna Mining lifts Diamba Sud Gold resource to 1.25M ounces
data.description:
• Fortuna Mining (FSM) (FVI:CA) on Thursday reported an updated mineral resource estimate for its Diamba Sud Gold project, increasing indicated resources to 1.25 million ounces of gold.
  • Fortuna is progressing with the feasibility study for the Diamba Sud Gold project to support a potential construction decision targeted for mid- 2026, with delivery of the study expected by the end of Q2 2026.
  • Ongoing exploration drilling of Southern Arc, Moungoundi, and additional new targets continues to intersect mineralization beyond the current mineral resource envelopes, and highlight further growth potential, the company said.

data.title: Merck reports positive phase 3 second-season RSV data for ENFLONSIA
data.description:
Merck & Co. (MRK), known as MSD outside the United States and Canada, on Thursday reported positive second-season results from the Phase 3 SMART trial evaluating ENFLONSIA (clesrovimab) in infants and children at increased risk for severe respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) disease.

New data from the SMART trial in children under 2 years of age who remained at increased risk for severe RSV disease through their second RSV season and received ENFLONSIA at the start of RSV season 2 showed that safety was generally consistent with safety observed in MK-1654-007 infants who received ENFLONSIA during RSV season 1, the company said.

Additionally, the monoclonal antibody (mAb) serum concentrations achieved in children under 2 years of age at increased risk for severe RSV disease through their second RSV season (secondary endpoint) were similar to those in healthy infants in the pivotal Phase 2b/3 CLEVER trial.

Results from the SMART study support extrapolation of efficacy to children under 2 years of age at increased risk for severe RSV disease through RSV season 2, Merck & Co. (MRK) said in a statement.

data.title: Amplitude outlines 15% 2026 revenue growth target as AI agents drive 25% of platform queries
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Amplitude (AMPL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Spenser Skates described Q4 as "one of the strongest quarters in Amplitude history," with revenue reaching $91.4 million, up 17% year-over-year, and annual recurring revenue (ARR) at $366 million. The CEO highlighted, "Our go-to-market motion has matured. There is a tighter focus on value-based cases in the enterprise and on expanding with multiproduct deployments."
  • Skates emphasized AI as a structural tailwind, stating, "Over the past 6 months, our Agentic analytics platform has reached a 76% success rate on complex production-grade queries, that is 7x better than a straight text-to-SQL approach."
  • The CEO introduced the launch of global AI agents and the MCP integration, noting, "Teams can now move from insight to action in minutes, not weeks using analytics, cohorts, experiments and messaging in one continuous agenetic workflow."
  • Skates also announced the acquisition of InfiniGrow, an AI-native marketing analytics startup, to strengthen the platform's capabilities.
  • CFO Andrew Casey stated, "The enterprise is now our core growth engine. ARR from our enterprise customer cohort is up 20% year-over-year, with higher retention and expansion rates than the rest of our business."

Outlook

  • Amplitude expects Q1 2026 revenue between $91.7 million and $93.7 million, representing an annual growth rate of 16% at the midpoint. For the full year 2026, revenue guidance is set between $390 million and $398 million—an annual growth rate of 15% at the midpoint.
  • Non-GAAP operating income for Q1 2026 is projected between (-$4.5 million) and (-$2.5 million). Full-year 2026 non-GAAP operating income is expected between $7 million and $13 million.
  • Non-GAAP net income per share guidance for Q1 2026 is between (-$0.02) and (-$0.01), and for the full year, between $0.08 and $0.13.
  • CFO Casey explained, "We are confident we have the right strategy and the right platform to continue to consolidate the fragmented market."

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was $91.4 million, up 17% year-over-year. Fiscal year 2025 revenue reached $343.2 million, a 15% year-over-year increase.
  • Total ARR was $366 million at quarter end, up $18 million sequentially.
  • Non-GAAP operating income for Q4 was $4.2 million, or 4.6% of revenue. Net income per share was $0.04 based on 141.5 million diluted shares.
  • Free cash flow in Q4 was $11.2 million or 12% of revenue, while full-year free cash flow set a record at nearly $24 million.
  • The number of customers with over $100,000 in ARR grew to 698, up 18% year-over-year; customers with over $1 million in ARR rose to 56, up 33% year-over-year.
  • Gross margin for Q4 was 77%, flat year-over-year and up 1 point sequentially.

Q&A

  • Taylor McGinnis, UBS: Asked about the evolution and differentiation between Amplitude's agents and third-party agents. CEO Skates responded, "They both use the same underlying infrastructure...the nature and type of questions are the same whether you're asking it from Claude or Slack or whether you're in Amplitudes UI."
  • McGinnis, UBS: Inquired about the lighter upside in the quarter. CFO Casey explained, "Q4 was a great quarter for new logo ARR...there was a large proportion of ARR that was booked later than we've seen in prior quarters."
  • William Fitzsimmons, Piper Sandler: Questioned NRR improvement drivers. CFO Casey noted, "The improvement in net dollar retention was related to our cross-sell capabilities."
  • Robert Oliver, RW Baird: Asked about the new pricing model's predictability. CFO Casey detailed, "Our new pricing and packaging structure rewards our customers now for adding more and more data into the platform so that they're paying marginally incrementally less."
  • Lucas Cerisola, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about new demand from smaller customers and enterprise strategy. CEO Skates stated, "We're doing both. The core thing you want...is the same, whether you're the newest start-up or your 100 year-old long-lasting business."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a constructive but probing tone, frequently seeking clarification on the sustainability of growth, pricing predictability, and the impact of AI-driven adoption. Questions often focused on expansion potential and competitive differentiation.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone, using phrases like "we are confident" and "we have a massive opportunity" both in prepared remarks and responses. The tone was more assertive regarding platform differentiation than previous quarters.
  • Compared to Q3, both management and analysts appeared slightly more bullish, with management increasingly emphasizing structural tailwinds from AI and analysts probing execution details and monetization strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated from Q3 to Q4, with sequential ARR increases rising from $12 million to $18 million.
  • The number of customers with over $100,000 in ARR increased more sharply quarter-over-quarter (up by 45 in Q4 vs. 19 in Q3).
  • Gross margin improved by 1 point sequentially, and free cash flow margin expanded significantly.
  • Management's tone shifted to highlight the success of multiproduct adoption and the launch of AI agents as a driver of future growth, while reinforcing structural improvements in pricing and enterprise focus.
  • Analysts' questions moved from early-stage AI adoption and product innovation in Q3 to scaling, pricing, and long-term platform strategy in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged continued competition from data platform players and the challenge of maintaining performance differentiation, with CEO Skates stating, "Customers always want the most advanced and bleeding edge capabilities."
  • Churn and contract rightsizing were identified as areas of focus, with improved NRR attributed to cross-sell and upsell motion.
  • CFO Casey mentioned, "We will have higher sales and marketing expenses as a percentage of revenue" in Q1 FY '26, reflecting timing of events.

Final Takeaway

Management highlighted one of the strongest quarters in company history, driven by a surge in enterprise adoption, rapid growth in AI agent-driven queries, and the successful launch of a simplified pricing model. The company expects structural tailwinds from AI to accelerate innovation, deepen customer engagement, and drive profitable growth in 2026, with guidance reflecting continued confidence in Amplitude's expanding platform opportunity.
